title: SQLite Database
documentation:
## Purpose
SQLite is a self-contained, serverless relational database engine stored as a single file. The [SQLite Database](https://capdag.com/browse/media:sqlite) media type represents any `.sqlite`, `.sqlite3`, or `.db` file that follows the SQLite file format.

## When to use
Use this type when a node produces or consumes a structured relational dataset that must remain portable as a single file artifact — for example, query results, local caches, or embedded application data stores. It is a better choice than CSV or JSON when the data has multiple tables, foreign keys, or requires SQL querying.

## Notes
Implementers should use the SQLite C library or a language binding to open files; do not treat them as raw binary blobs. Consumers that only need read access should open the database in read-only mode to avoid unintended writes or WAL file creation.
